sections.paragraph:
κατασύρω fut. 3 sg. κατασυρεῖ Da 11:26 LXX; aor. κατέσυρα Jer 30:4 (s. σύρω; Hdt. et al.; LXX; Jos., Bell. 2, 190; Test12Patr) drag (away by force) (Parthenius 19; Dio Chrys. 1, 196; Philo, In Flacc. 174, Leg. ad Gai. 131) τινά someone πρὸς τὸν κριτήν before the judge Lk 12:58. DELG s.v. σύρω.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Parthenius Parthenius , I b.c.—List 5

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 528
